# actions-to-mcp

> Migration guide for moving Sema4.ai custom actions to remote MCP servers. Audience: technical users who already ship Sema4.ai Action packs. Fluency with Python, uv, OAuth, and HTTP is assumed.

Sema4.ai supports only remote MCPs using streamable-HTTP transport. Stdio MCPs are useful for local development but cannot be registered with a Sema4.ai agent. The biggest conceptual shift is orchestration: with Action Server, the Sema4.ai platform called your code inside the agent runtime. With MCPs, the Sema4.ai agent calls your server over HTTP and picks which tool to use from the tool's name and description — tool metadata is the new contract. The guide assumes customers do the migration from a coding agent, and treats Claude Code as the default throughout.

## Shipped

- [README](README.md): Landing page. Remote-only callout, four-step decision preview, repo map, status table.
- [docs/01-decide.md](docs/01-decide.md): Ordered decision tree for each action. Four outcomes — use a vendor MCP, extract as an SDM Verified Query, delete, or migrate to MCP. SDM is the only non-MCP alternative in scope.
- [docs/02-mental-model.md](docs/02-mental-model.md): Six conceptual shifts from Action Server to MCP — remote-only, HTTP calls from the Sema4.ai agent, context via X-Tool-Invocation-Context, auth as request context, streamable-HTTP transport, vendor-first. Concept-to-equivalent reference table at the bottom.
- [docs/03-framework-and-setup.md](docs/03-framework-and-setup.md): Framework (fastmcp), Python + uv, layout, minimum server.py, Claude Code as the coding agent, MCP Inspector for parity checks, ngrok-to-Sema4.ai for full-loop local dev, and a short paragraph on registering the MCP with a Sema4.ai agent (URL, auth config, common gotchas).
- [docs/04-migration-workflow.md](docs/04-migration-workflow.md): End-to-end migration loop driven by Claude Code plus the convert-action-pack skill — prerequisites, inventory review, auth classification, scaffold, MCP Inspector spot-check, ngrok-to-Sema4.ai full-loop test, deploy.
- [.claude/skills/convert-action-pack/SKILL.md](.claude/skills/convert-action-pack/SKILL.md): Self-contained Claude Code skill. Inventory, @query→SDM extraction, auth × context × thread-files coverage matrix, inlined snippets (X-Tool-Invocation-Context parser, ContextVar binding, sema4ai-api-client file helpers), target layout, tests, pitfalls, validation gate, parity report.
- [examples/worked-migration/](examples/worked-migration/): Microsoft SharePoint action pack → MCP, commit by commit, applying the convert-action-pack skill. Includes a runnable scaffold (microsoft-sharepoint-mcp/) with all 12 tools, helpers, and tests; a legacy-snapshot of key excerpts from the source pack; and a parity report with the lessons learned.
- [examples/agent-server-example/](examples/agent-server-example/): Focused, runnable reference for the Sema4.ai platform-feature surface itself — invocation context, thread dataframes, and thread files — as six MCP tools backed by sema4ai-api-client. Not a migration of a specific pack; the companion to docs/05 when you need to see the context header, Agent Server callbacks, and overlay files fit together. Flat layout with smoke + context tests.
- [examples/feedback-mcp/](examples/feedback-mcp/): From-scratch reference MCP (not a migration) that captures in-run 👍/👎 feedback and demonstrates the data-connection pattern — resolving a Postgres connection from the agent's SDM data connection via the callback token, so the MCP needs no DB secret. Three tools (give_thumbs_up / give_thumbs_down / add_feedback_reason), a runbook segment with sema4-json quick-options, migrations, a read-only reporting SDM (reporter-agent/), tests, and two consumer agents in example-agents/ (rate-my-food-recommendation produces feedback; agent-feedback-analyzer reports on it).
- [docs/05-sema4-patterns.md](docs/05-sema4-patterns.md): Single-page reference for Sema4.ai-specific integration — X-Tool-Invocation-Context header (wire format, fields, direct-parse vs ContextVar-overlay patterns), auth (forwarded bearer, fastmcp JWTVerifier, API-key with multi-value secrets, OAuth scope consolidation), thread-file callbacks via sema4ai-api-client. Points to the convert-action-pack skill and the SharePoint worked migration for code.
- [docs/06-tools-and-testing.md](docs/06-tools-and-testing.md): Combined reference — MCP tool design (naming, descriptions, ToolAnnotations, Pydantic I/O, errors, pagination, granularity, consistency) and testing (three-layer strategy smoke → tool → parity, context tests for overlays, parity tactics via MCP Inspector + side-by-side runs + ngrok full-loop, parity checklist, what-not-to-test). Points to the SharePoint example throughout.
- [docs/07-orchestration/](docs/07-orchestration/): Four-page directory — Cloud Run, Bedrock AgentCore, Azure Container Apps, plus a multi-MCP gateway pattern for hosting several migrated MCPs behind one endpoint. Each page covers container shape, deploy command, secrets, session affinity; registration lives in the 03-framework-and-setup paragraph.
- [docs/08-observability.md](docs/08-observability.md): Thin pointer — per-platform native tooling (Cloud Logging, CloudWatch, Log Analytics) handles logs and traces; your MCP just needs to log tool name + duration + request IDs, and redact tokens / file bytes / full context headers. OpenTelemetry is optional.
- [docs/09-faq.md](docs/09-faq.md): Frequently asked questions — must-migrate-every-action, stdio vs remote, mixed @action+@query packs, chat.* APIs, cold starts, versioning, ngrok pre-deploy testing, vendor MCPs landing later, laptop production, rate limiting, multi-auth, Python version, tool-description quality, output-shape drift.

## Skills and artifacts for agents

- .claude/skills/analyze-agent-zip/ (shipped): Read-only triage step that runs BEFORE convert-action-pack. Takes an exported Sema4.ai agent (.zip bundling the runbook + one or more action packs), unpacks it, and produces a migration PLAN — a per-pack decision (use a vendor MCP / extract as SDM Verified Queries / delete / migrate to MCP), per-pack inventory tables, and a consolidated customer-requirements checklist (secrets, OAuth apps + scopes + admin consent, data connections). Writes no code; hands each migrate-bound pack to convert-action-pack.
- .claude/skills/convert-action-pack/ (shipped): Takes a Sema4.ai Action pack, produces an inventory table, extracts @query functions as SDM Verified Queries, classifies auth per tool, and scaffolds a fastmcp server with tests. Built for Claude Code; content is portable markdown usable from other coding agents.
